</mxCell> <mxCell id="L4tNkNG3bc7pMyOVOOL1-13" parent="1" style="rhombus;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<div>Balanced?</div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="80" x="388" y="400" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="L4tNkNG3bc7pMyOVOOL1-17" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;" value="<p>If Test 2 was unbalanced, the imbalance is in the set AFCEB.</p> <p>By comparing the outcome of Test 2 to Test 1, we can determine whether the<br> imbalance is in the set ACE or FB. If the seesaw side which fell is the same<br> from Test 1 to Test 2, we know the imbalance did not change sides, and therefore<br> lies in set ACE. If the falling seesaw side in Test 2 is opposite Test 1, we <br> know the imbalance changed sides, and is therefore in set FB. Based on this,<br> the user should either test one from FB against a safe individual, or split<br> the two sharing a side from ACE, and test them against each other.</p> <p>Note here, this particular outcome is why we did not use 6 of the 8 potential<br> islanders when performing Test 2. If we had done that, a possible set containing<br> the imbalanced islander would be ACEG. It is not possible to determine the source<br> of the imbalance from four possibilities with one remaining test.</p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="390" width="315" x="513" y="10" as="geometry" /> this set which have shared a side and test them<br> against each other.</p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="130" width="160" x="23" y="350" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.p: If Test 1 was unbalanced, the imbalance lies in the set ABCDEFGH.
Take 5 of the 8 potential, reverse the positions of 2, and add a safe individual.
By reversing the positions of 2 islanders, we are implementing idea(3).

path.style: stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));
fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 432px; margin-left: 309px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; background-color: #ffffff;

display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; background-color: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); white-space: nowrap;

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 313px; height: 1px; padding-top: 197px; margin-left: 492px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.p: If Test 2 was unbalanced, the imbalance is in the set AFCEB.

By comparing the outcome of Test 2 to Test 1, we can determine whether the
imbalance is in the set ACE or FB. If the seesaw side which fell is the same
from Test 1 to Test 2, we know the imbalance did not change sides, and therefore
lies in set ACE. If the falling seesaw side in Test 2 is opposite Test 1, we
know the imbalance changed sides, and is therefore in set FB. Based on this,
the user should either test one from FB against a safe individual, or split
the two sharing a side from ACE, and test them against each other.

Note here, this particular outcome is why we did not use 6 of the 8 potential
islanders when performing Test 2. If we had done that, a possible set containing
the imbalanced islander would be ACEG. It is not possible to determine the source
of the imbalance from four possibilities with one remaining test.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 158px; height: 1px; padding-top: 349px; margin-left: 2px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.p: If Test 2 was balanced, the imbalance is in the untested set, DGH.

Take the two from this set which have shared a side and test them
against each other.

path.style: stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));
